---

Let's face it, everybody's got two cents and everybody's got two minutes. So here's my two cents in under two minutes. Herman Cain is in the news. Herman Cain is being accused of being unfaithful to his wife and having to settle charges that are over 12 years old against him about promiscuity. Now, Herman's getting in trouble right now because he's not correctly handling a situation. When you have a problem like this, you need to be proactive. Hit it hard, hit it fast, and be on offense. When a customer has a complaint against you or your company, you need to not ignore it, not avoid it. Look, the world abhors a vacuum. If Herman's gonna leave a vacuum, the media's gonna fill it in. So my suggestion in these grant rants, what we do is we take the news, all you need to know about the news, and try to twist it to where you and I can learn from it rather than be distracted by it. Herman needs to come out and say, look, I am faithful husband for 38 years. What happens between my wife and my marriage is between me and her. We're in completely good standings. These two issues that happened are not uncommon to happen to people in business where there's misunderstandings. These were settled. Everybody was satisfied with the settlements. It happens in business. My marriage is perfect. I'm running for president of the United States. I expect attacks like this, and this is the last time I'm gonna be handling this situation that was handled over 12 years ago. See if Herman took a position like that, and I'm not taking a position whether Herman should be president, whether this should derail his campaign, which I don't think it will. I'm not taking a position on that. I'm using this as a point to say, how am I gonna handle it when I have a complaint against me, my clients, or my company, or my people? When we make a mistake, legitimate mistake, how are we gonna handle that? How do you handle it quickly? Fill in the void, fill in the vacuum, be on offense. If you need to apologize, apologize, and get on with your life. Be proactive.
